title: Setting Up R, RStudio, & klct
text: Download R install . require administrator privileges. https://cloud.r-project.org/bin/windows/base/

title: Setting Up R, RStudio, & klct
text: Likewise, download RStudio install . also require administrator privileges. https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/

title: Setting Up R, RStudio, & klct
text: need make sure tidyverse R package installed. ’ll also use remotes package install klct, need installed well.

title: Setting Up R, RStudio, & klct
text: Click Install Packages pane:  , type tidyverse remotes, click Install:

title: Setting Up R, RStudio, & klct
text: Alternatively, can type following R console:

title: Setting Up R, RStudio, & klct
text: Since klct package development CRAN, must installed differently. Type following R console:

title: Create and Run a Standard microCT Analysis
text: first step create folder contain data analysis. First, open RStudio. , create new RStudio project: File > New Project... Select New Directory:  Select New Project:  Type name folder like create, select location like create folder. click Create Project:  now new folder contains .Rproj file name project possibly .Rproj.user folder.  safe ignore, don’t delete move .

title: Create and Run a Standard microCT Analysis
text: Create subfolder called data inside project folder. Place following CSV files data folder: mouse-table.csv – lookup table defining sample’s Animal ID, (key registry) number, sex, treatment (required). spine_trabecular_data.csv – Trabecular bone data lumbar vertebra. femur_metaphysis_data.csv – Combined cortical trabecular bone data femoral metaphysis. femur_diaphysis_data.csv – Cortical bone data femoral diaphysis. project folder now look something like :  files technically can renamed whatever want, update file names analysis report. ’ll show .

title: Create and Run a Standard microCT Analysis
text: mouse table CSV file maps animal’s number sex treatment (genotype). columns Animal ID (optional), , Sex, Treatment (Genotype). example:  Sex can coded Female/Male, F/M, female/male — klct standardize F M.

title: Create and Run a Standard microCT Analysis
text: data CSV files created neoscan-data-assembler software tool, assembles CSV files tab-separated text files produced NeoWorks program. file contain column matches numbers mouse table, along bone measurement columns. example, spine trabecular data file might look like :

title: Create and Run a Standard microCT Analysis
text: Click Addins button top RStudio:  klct heading, select template matches analysis design: NeoScan Two Treatment Comparison Setup — Compares two treatment groups sex analyzed separately. NeoScan Two Treatment Two Sex Comparison Setup — Runs full two-way (Treatment × Sex) analysis. Mayo-themed versions template also available.  now new .Rmd file labeled today’s date project folder:  Alternatively, can generate template R console: Mayo-themed templates, add mayo = TRUE:
code: library(klct) create_two_treatment_comparison() # or create_two_treatment_two_sex_comparison() create_two_treatment_comparison(mayo = TRUE) # or create_two_treatment_two_sex_comparison(mayo = TRUE)

title: Create and Run a Standard microCT Analysis
text: Open .Rmd file RStudio. can clicking file name Files pane. .Rmd file just text file, now open editing within RStudio:

title: Create and Run a Standard microCT Analysis
text: top file, replace Template Title title ’d like give report, Name name, Date today’s date.

title: Create and Run a Standard microCT Analysis
text: title information, params section contains file paths data files: renamed files placed somewhere data folder, update file paths .

title: Create and Run a Standard microCT Analysis
text: exclude specific animals analysis, add numbers outliers parameter. Outliers site-specific, can exclude different animals skeletal site: excluded outliers, may wish include explanation (e.g., broken metaphysis). Scroll see: Add explanation empty space START OUTLIER INFO END OUTLIER INFO.
code: outliers: !r |         list(             spine = c(1234, 5678),             met = c(),             dia = c(1234)         ) <!-- START OUTLIER INFO --> <!-- Describe the reason(s) for excluding outliers, if relevant (e.g., broken met). -->   <!-- END OUTLIER INFO -->

title: Create and Run a Standard microCT Analysis
text: default, groups ordered alphabetically. set specific order (e.g., make control group appear first), list group names groups parameter:

title: Create and Run a Standard microCT Analysis
text: measures parameter controls bone parameters analyzed: "default" – Analyze sensible subset commonly reported parameters (default). "" – Analyze detected parameters.

title: Create and Run a Standard microCT Analysis
text: Optionally, add background information project first section document, START BACKGROUND INFO END BACKGROUND INFO. appear top final report. Don’t change anything else lower file.
code: <!-- START BACKGROUND INFO --> <!-- Describe the experiment, genotypes/treatments, and hypotheses here. -->   <!-- END BACKGROUND INFO -->

title: Create and Run a Standard microCT Analysis
text: Make sure file saved pressing Ctrl + S clicking Save button:  Now, generate report clicking Knit (pressing Ctrl + Shift + K):  seconds, final report appear Viewer pane. report contains tabbed sections skeletal site, separate tabs parametric nonparametric results, plots, raw data:  final report now .html file working folder:  can anything regular file .html file: save anywhere like, attach email, etc. can also double click open default web browser easier viewing.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Richard Lindsey. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Lindsey R (2026). klct: Analyze NeoScan microCT Data. R package version 0.1.0, https://khoslalab.github.io/klct/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {klct: Analyze NeoScan microCT Data},   author = {Richard Lindsey},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://khoslalab.github.io/klct/}, }

title: Analyze NeoScan microCT Data
text: package contains functions read analyze microCT data NeoScan scanner currently performed Khosla Lab. Sites analyzed include femoral metaphysis, femoral diaphysis, lumbar vertebra. Analyses include trabecular bone parameters cortical bone parameters. parametric (t-test, two-way ANOVA) nonparametric (Wilcoxon rank-sum, Aligned Rank Transform via ARTool) tests supported. Comparisons can made two treatments (sex analyzed separately), two treatments two sexes full two-way design. Measures detected dynamically data, measures parameter lets analyze sensible defaults (NULL), detected columns (""), custom set column names. Mayo-themed R Markdown templates available via mayodown package.

title: Analyze NeoScan microCT Data
text: can install development version klct GitHub :

title: Analyze NeoScan microCT Data
text: Tb.BV/TV Tb.Th Tb.Sp Tb.N Tb.BV/TV Tb.Th Tb.Sp Tb.N
code: library(klct)  mouse_table <- read_mouse_table(klct_ex("mouse-table.csv"))  spine_trab <- read_trabecular(klct_ex("spine_trabecular_data.csv"),                               mouse_table, site = "Spine")  # Parametric comparison (default) Spine.Tb <- compare_groups(spine_trab)  # Nonparametric comparison Spine.Tb_np <- compare_groups(spine_trab, test = "nonparametric")  # All available trabecular measures Spine.Tb_all <- compare_groups(spine_trab, measures = "all")  # Two-way (Treatment x Sex) comparison Spine.Tb_2x2 <- compare_groups_2x2(spine_trab) print_results(Spine.Tb)

title: Analyze NeoScan microCT Data
text: Four R Markdown templates available RStudio template menu (File > New File > R Markdown > Template), via RStudio Addins (Addins > klct) via helper functions: Parametric nonparametric results displayed separate tabs within section, Combined Sexes Plots tabs included sections.
code: # Standard templates create_two_treatment_comparison() create_two_treatment_two_sex_comparison()  # Mayo-themed templates (uses mayodown::mayohtml) create_two_treatment_comparison(mayo = TRUE) create_two_treatment_two_sex_comparison(mayo = TRUE)

what: Compare microCT data between two treatments — compare_groups
title: Compare microCT data between two treatments — compare_groups
text: function takes trabecular cortical bone microCT data one site compares two treatments. default, sex compared separately. parametric (Student's t-test) nonparametric (Wilcoxon rank-sum test) approach can selected.

title: Compare microCT data between two treatments — compare_groups
code: compare_groups(data, type = NULL, measures = NULL, test = "parametric")

title: Compare microCT data between two treatments — compare_groups
text: data Bone microCT data data frame, formatted output read_trabecular() read_cortical(). Note data one Site supplied time. type string indicating type data supplied. Options include "trabecular" "cortical". Defaults NULL, case function try figure type data . measures bone parameters analyze. three options: NULL (default): analyze sensible subset commonly reported parameters. trabecular data stored default_trabecular_measures; cortical data, default_cortical_measures. "": analyze every detected parameter appropriate type. character vector specific column names (e.g., c("Tb.BV/TV", "Tb.Th")). test string indicating statistical test use. Options "parametric" (default) Student's t-test, "nonparametric" Wilcoxon rank-sum test.

title: Compare microCT data between two treatments — compare_groups
text: list bone measure analyzed. measure list sex analyzed. sex tibble/data frame containing columns Sex, Treatment, n, Mean, SEM, P, Sig (string containing "*" P < 0.05, "**" P < 0.01, "***" P < 0.001). test = "nonparametric", Mean replaced Median SEM IQR.

title: Compare microCT data between two treatments — compare_groups
code: mouse_table <- read_mouse_table(klct_ex("mouse-table.csv")) spine_trab <- read_trabecular(klct_ex("spine_trabecular_data.csv"),                               mouse_table, site = "Spine")  # Default measures, parametric spine_res <- compare_groups(spine_trab)  # All available trabecular measures spine_all <- compare_groups(spine_trab, measures = "all")  # Specific measures, nonparametric spine_custom <- compare_groups(spine_trab,                                measures = c("Tb.BV/TV", "Tb.Th"),                                test = "nonparametric")

what: Compare microCT data between two treatments and two sexes — compare_groups_2x2
title: Compare microCT data between two treatments and two sexes — compare_groups_2x2
text: function takes trabecular cortical bone microCT data one site compares using two-way design Treatment Sex factors. parametric (two-way ANOVA) nonparametric (Aligned Rank Transform via ARTool package) approach can selected.

title: Compare microCT data between two treatments and two sexes — compare_groups_2x2
code: compare_groups_2x2(data, type = NULL, measures = NULL, test = "parametric")

title: Compare microCT data between two treatments and two sexes — compare_groups_2x2
text: data Bone microCT data data frame, formatted output read_trabecular() read_cortical(). Note data one Site supplied time. type string indicating type data supplied. Options include "trabecular" "cortical". Defaults NULL, case function try figure type data . measures bone parameters analyze. three options: NULL (default): analyze sensible subset commonly reported parameters. trabecular data stored default_trabecular_measures; cortical data, default_cortical_measures. "": analyze every detected parameter appropriate type. character vector specific column names (e.g., c("Tb.BV/TV", "Tb.Th")). test string indicating statistical test use. Options "parametric" (default) Student's t-test, "nonparametric" Wilcoxon rank-sum test.

title: Compare microCT data between two treatments and two sexes — compare_groups_2x2
text: list bone measure analyzed. measure list two elements: omnibus test table: anova (parametric) art_anova (nonparametric), containing columns Factor, P, Sig. summary: tibble group-level summary statistics pairwise comparisons (t-test parametric, Wilcoxon nonparametric) merged . Contains Sex, Treatment/Genotype, n, Mean/SEM (Median/IQR), P, Sig. Within sex first group row P = NA Sig = "", second group row carries pairwise P-value significance stars.

title: Compare microCT data between two treatments and two sexes — compare_groups_2x2
code: mouse_table <- read_mouse_table(klct_ex("mouse-table.csv")) spine_trab <- read_trabecular(klct_ex("spine_trabecular_data.csv"),                               mouse_table, site = "Spine")  # Default measures spine_2x2 <- compare_groups_2x2(spine_trab)  # All available trabecular measures spine_2x2_all <- compare_groups_2x2(spine_trab, measures = "all")

what: Create a project skeleton to compare bone microCT data — create_two_treatment_comparison
title: Create a project skeleton to compare bone microCT data — create_two_treatment_comparison
text: functions assume project root directory create R Markdown file containing pre-filled bone microCT analysis. create analysis R Markdown file using appropriate package template.

title: Create a project skeleton to compare bone microCT data — create_two_treatment_comparison
code: create_two_treatment_comparison(   file_name = paste0(format(Sys.Date(), "%F"), "-microCT-Analysis.Rmd"),   mayo = FALSE )  create_two_treatment_two_sex_comparison(   file_name = paste0(format(Sys.Date(), "%F"), "-microCT-Analysis.Rmd"),   mayo = FALSE )

title: Create a project skeleton to compare bone microCT data — create_two_treatment_comparison
text: file_name string name project analysis R Markdown document. Defaults file style "YYYY-MM-DD-microCT-Analysis.Rmd", YYYY-MM-DD represents today's date. mayo Logical. TRUE, create Mayo-themed template using mayodown::mayohtml. FALSE (default), use rmarkdown::html_document.

title: Create a project skeleton to compare bone microCT data — create_two_treatment_comparison
text: Returns TRUE successful.

title: Create a project skeleton to compare bone microCT data — create_two_treatment_comparison
text: create_two_treatment_comparison() creates template comparing two treatment groups, analyzing sex separately. create_two_treatment_two_sex_comparison() creates template full two-way (Treatment x Sex) analysis. functions accept mayo argument. mayo = TRUE, template uses mayodown::mayohtml Mayo-themed report. mayo = FALSE (default), template uses rmarkdown::html_document.

title: Create a project skeleton to compare bone microCT data — create_two_treatment_comparison
code: if (FALSE) { create_two_treatment_comparison() create_two_treatment_comparison(mayo = TRUE) create_two_treatment_two_sex_comparison() create_two_treatment_two_sex_comparison(mayo = TRUE) }

what: Plot bone microCT data by treatment — plot_groups
title: Plot bone microCT data by treatment — plot_groups
text: function takes bone microCT data produces boxplots comparing two treatments. sex plotted separately.

title: Plot bone microCT data by treatment — plot_groups
code: plot_groups(   data,   type = NULL,   measures = NULL,   test = "parametric",   title = "sex",   x_axis_angle = 45 )

title: Plot bone microCT data by treatment — plot_groups
text: data tibble/data frame containing bone microCT data, formatted output read_trabecular() read_cortical(). type string indicating type data supplied. Options include "trabecular" "cortical". Defaults NULL, case function try figure type data . measures bone parameters plot. three options: NULL (default): plot sensible subset commonly reported parameters defined default_trabecular_measures default_cortical_measures. "": plot every detected parameter appropriate type. character vector specific column names (e.g., c("Ct.Ar", "Ct.Th")). test string indicating statistical test use significance annotations. Options "parametric" (default) Student's t-test, "nonparametric" Wilcoxon rank-sum test. title string indicating type title plots . Defaults "sex", currently option implemented. remove titles plots, set title NULL. x_axis_angle angle rotate x-axis labels overlap, passed ggplot2::theme() axis.text.x = element_text(angle = x_axis_angle).

title: Plot bone microCT data by treatment — plot_groups
text: list containing ggplot2::ggplot() objects sex analyzed. print plot without printing list index, see print_plots().

title: Plot bone microCT data by treatment — plot_groups
text: two treatments significantly different, statistical significance indicated "*" P < 0.05, "**" P < 0.01, "***" P < 0.001.

title: Plot bone microCT data by treatment — plot_groups
code: mouse_table <- read_mouse_table(klct_ex("mouse-table.csv")) met_cort <- read_cortical(klct_ex("femur_metaphysis_data.csv"),                           mouse_table, site = "Met")  # Default measures plot_groups(met_cort) |> print_plots()    # All cortical measures plot_groups(met_cort, measures = "all") |> print_plots()    # Specific measures, nonparametric plot_groups(met_cort, measures = c("Ct.Th", "Ct.vBMD"),             test = "nonparametric") |> print_plots()
